primary_concern: Sepsis with multi-organ dysfunction and acute kidney injury
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L), consistent with tissue hypoperfusion. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation criteria. Concurrent findings include anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL), elevated WBC (14.2 K/uL), and bilirubin (1.4 mg/dL), suggesting systemic inflammation. AKI stage 1-3 progression is documented with creatinine peaks (excluded outlier at 8.9 mg/dL). No clinical notes confirm infection source.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
Creatinine elevation with AKI stage progression (Stage 1 to Stage 3) documented over 48 hours
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy criteria and increases bleeding risk
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements and fluid resuscitation status unknown

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection source workup
Assess for AKI reversibility factors (e.g., hypovolemia, nephrotoxins)
Consider platelet transfusion if <50 K/uL or active bleeding
Monitor for septic shock progression (MAP, lactate trends, fluid responsiveness)
doctor_handoff: Review for infection source (urine, imaging, clinical exam). Confirm AKI etiology (prerenal vs intrinsic). Assess need for vasopressors if hypotension develops. Validate creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for measurement accuracy.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL excluded as outlier), Platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
